.codex/agents.purpose: Codex CLI agent definitions for the Archie workflow. Holds archie-analysis.toml, the worker-agent profile used for Archie scan, deep-scan, and intent-layer subagent tasks. Config-only — no executable source.

patterns.name: Single worker-agent TOML profile
patterns.description: An agent is one .toml file declaring name, description, model_reasoning_effort, sandbox_mode, and a developer_instructions block. The archie_analysis worker runs with medium reasoning effort and workspace-write sandbox.

patterns.name: Constrained worker contract in developer_instructions
patterns.description: developer_instructions pin the worker to the parent prompt: stay within the assigned task, write the requested artifact to the requested path, do not modify unrelated files, and do not paste large artifacts back into the conversation.
patterns.example: developer_instructions = """You are an Archie workflow worker. Follow the parent prompt exactly... Do not paste large artifacts..."""

key_file_guides.role: Defines the archie_analysis worker agent used by Archie scan/deep-scan/intent-layer subagent tasks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: sandbox_mode is workspace-write — the worker may write artifacts but must not touch unrelated source; keep developer_instructions aligned with the Archie subagent contract.

anti_patterns: Loosening sandbox_mode beyond workspace-write for the analysis worker
Adding business or repo-specific logic into the agent profile (it only configures a worker)
Editing developer_instructions to allow modifying unrelated source files or pasting large artifacts

.codex/rules.purpose: Codex execpolicy rules that auto-approve the shell commands Archie's deep-scan / intent-layer / share / viewer workflows invoke, so the user is not prompted mid-run. Generated config — produced by CodexConnector.finalize() at install time.

patterns.name: prefix_rule allow-list per Archie script
patterns.description: Each Archie helper (.archie/*.py) gets a prefix_rule with decision="allow", a justification, and explicit match exemplars for both bare and "$PROJECT_ROOT" invocations.
patterns.example: prefix_rule(pattern = ["python3", ".archie/scanner.py"], decision = "allow", justification = "Run Archie's scanner.py", match = ["python3 .archie/scanner.py", "python3 .archie/scanner.py \"$PROJECT_ROOT\""])

patterns.name: Most-restrictive-wins precedence
patterns.description: Forbidden > prompt > allow, so any stricter user rule in the project still overrides these allow entries. These rules only widen approval for known-safe Archie commands.

patterns.name: Read-only general tooling allowed
patterns.description: Beyond Archie scripts, only read-only/transient commands are allowed: cat, ls, wc, head, sort, date, echo, test, mkdir, cp, git read subcommands (status/diff/log/rev-parse/ls-files/ls-tree/show, git -C), and rm -f scoped to .archie artifacts.

patterns.name: Justified broad-prefix exceptions
patterns.description: A few unavoidably broad prefixes (python3 -c, codex exec, rm -f, git -C) carry inline justifications explaining why argv-prefix matching cannot bind tighter.
patterns.example: prefix_rule(pattern = ["codex", "exec"], decision = "allow", justification = "verify_findings.py spawns `codex exec` ... during Step 9 drift verification")

key_file_guides.role: Generated execpolicy allow-list (developers.openai.com/codex/rules format) covering every Archie workflow shell command.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated by CodexConnector.finalize() from manifest COMMAND_RULES + _STANDALONE_SCRIPTS — hand edits are overwritten on reinstall; rm -f and git -C are intentionally broad because bash glob/dir expansion prevents tighter argv binding.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing archie.rules instead of updating the Archie manifest (changes are overwritten on reinstall)
Adding decision="allow" for write-capable or arbitrary commands beyond the read-only/transient set
Broadening rm -f or git -C beyond Archie's own .archie/ paths and read-only git subcommands

decisions.decision: Auto-approve only known Archie workflow commands, leaving precedence to stricter user rules
decisions.rationale: Avoids mid-run prompts during long deep-scan/intent-layer runs while keeping most-restrictive-wins so project-level forbid/prompt rules still apply.

api/client/go.purpose: Generated Go SDK for the OpenMeter v1 REST API. client.gen.go is fully generated by oapi-codegen from api/openapi.cloud.yaml; client.go adds ergonomic wrappers and auth helpers on top. A public SDK — must remain importable without the full monorepo.

patterns.name: Never edit client.gen.go
patterns.description: client.gen.go carries the oapi-codegen DO NOT EDIT header and is regenerated by make gen-api; add wrappers to client.go instead.
patterns.example: // add wrapper method to client.go, not client.gen.go

patterns.name: Ergonomic wrappers in client.go only
patterns.description: client.go is the only hand-written Go file; add only methods that delegate to verbose generated method names — no type definitions or business logic.
patterns.example: func (c *ClientWithResponses) IngestEventWithResponse(ctx, event, reqEditors...) (*IngestEventsResponse, error) { return c.IngestEventsWithApplicationCloudeventsPlusJSONBodyWithResponse(ctx, event, reqEditors...) }

patterns.name: Auth via RequestEditorFn option
patterns.description: NewAuthClient(WithResponses) prepend a WithRequestEditorFn that sets Authorization: Bearer, then append caller-supplied opts; auth is never embedded in generated code.
patterns.example: WithRequestEditorFn(func(ctx, req *http.Request) error { req.Header.Set("Authorization", fmt.Sprintf("Bearer %s", apiSecret)); return nil })

patterns.name: Tests use httptest.NewServer mock
patterns.description: client_test.go validates each wrapper against an httptest.Server, asserting method, path, Content-Type, and comparing decoded structs with assert.Equal.
patterns.example: server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w, r) { assert.Equal(t, http.MethodPost, r.Method); assert.Equal(t, "/api/v1/events", r.URL.Path) }))

patterns.name: Enum Valid() for runtime validation
patterns.description: Generated enums (AddonStatus, AppType, etc.) have a Valid() bool switch over all constants; use it instead of manual switches.
patterns.example: func (e AppType) Valid() bool { switch e { case AppTypeCustomInvoicing, AppTypeSandbox, AppTypeStripe: return true; default: return false } }

key_file_guides.role: Fully generated SDK: all types, enums, request/response structs, and HTTP methods from api/openapi.cloud.yaml via codegen.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not edit; regenerate with make gen-api. Uses always-prefix-enum-values and disable-required-readonly-as-pointer — changing codegen.yaml flags affects all enum names and pointer semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written ergonomic layer: auth constructors (NewAuthClient(WithResponses)) and CloudEvents ingest shorthands (IngestEvent/Batch + WithResponse variants); holds the //go:generate directive.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only add methods delegating to generated methods — no type definitions or business logic.

key_file_guides.role: oapi-codegen config: package 'openmeter', generates client + models + embedded-spec into client.gen.go with always-prefix-enum-values.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing compatibility flags affects all enum constant names and pointer semantics across the SDK; coordinate with api/openapi.cloud.yaml changes.

key_file_guides.role: White-box (package openmeter) unit tests for the hand-written wrappers using mock HTTP servers; imports openmeter/meter for MeterAggregation casting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TetsListEvents (note the typo) is never run by go test — the missing Test prefix means the function is silently skipped; do not replicate the pattern.

key_file_guides.role: ErrResponse struct (StatusCode, StatusText, AppCode, Message) for rendering HTTP error responses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Separate from generated error types in client.gen.go; ErrResponse is for rendering, not domain errors.

anti_patterns: Editing client.gen.go manually — overwritten on every make gen-api run
Adding new API types or request/response structs to client.go — they belong in api/spec/ TypeSpec
Embedding auth tokens/credentials directly in client methods instead of ClientOption/RequestEditorFn
Importing app-internal monorepo packages beyond those already referenced — this is a public SDK
Writing tests that bypass httptest.NewServer and make live HTTP calls

decisions.rationale: The cloud variant includes cloud-specific endpoints; enum prefixing prevents collisions when enums share value strings (e.g. 'active').

decisions.decision: Ergonomic wrappers in client.go rather than custom codegen templates
decisions.rationale: CloudEvents ingest has two content-types producing verbose generated names; short wrappers improve DX without forking the generator.

decisions.decision: Auth injected via RequestEditorFn option chain
decisions.rationale: Keeps generated code immutable and lets callers compose multiple editors (auth + tracing + retry) without modifying SDK source.
